$doc: Loop driver for Lede v2 (provider-agnostic/BYOK/auth/Drizzle spec).
Phase 0 detailed (it IS the whole risk, §24); Phases 1-4 coarse epics,
decomposed as Phase 0 teaches the shape. Ready/batches/breaches come from
schedule.mjs, never by eye. files is a CONTRACT (verifier fails undeclared
touches; package.json + bun.lock allowlisted). Since nothing type-checks
without T001's tsconfig, every ticket transitively depends on T001.
Acceptance hardened by the intake red-team (ledger [v2-004]) — contrast
checks, not existence checks. Two structural holes closed: (A) fixture
provenance via the shared eval-core T016 + the Phase-0 live-reproduces-
fixtures gate; (B) tag-scoring via the tag-shuffle invariance control.

tickets.title: Replace Bun scaffold with Fastify + Vite/React skeleton (bun toolchain)
tickets.context: Establish the §21 layout on the LOCKED stack (§3.1) but with the BUN toolchain (oracle.md 'Toolchain'): Fastify 4 API + Vite/React 18 SPA, one process. DELETE the existing Bun.serve scaffold (src/main.ts, src/routes/health.ts) — replaced, do not keep Bun.serve. Set up: tsconfig (client, ESM, react-jsx, path alias @shared/* → src/shared/*, include src/**), tsconfig.server.json, vite.config.ts (react plugin, @shared alias, dev proxy /api → http://localhost:8787), index.html mounting src/client/main.tsx (bare App shell), src/client/styles/{app.css,print.css} stubs. src/server/index.ts: minimal Fastify server booting on PORT (default 8787, §17) serving GET /api/health → {ok:true} (in prod also serves dist/ static — TODO stub fine). package.json scripts (bun): dev (concurrently dev:api+dev:web), dev:api (bun --watch src/server/index.ts), dev:web (vite), build (vite build), check (tsc --noEmit both configs), start (bun src/server/index.ts), test (vitest run). Deps per §20 (fastify, react, react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, vitest, concurrently, typescript). Locked: PORT 8787, Fastify not Bun.serve, ESM, @shared.

- `bun install` succeeds; `bun run check` passes; `bun run build` emits dist/index.html
- server boots (`bun run start`); `curl localhost:8787/api/health` served by the running Fastify instance → `{"ok":true}`
- src/main.ts & src/routes/health.ts DO NOT exist; `grep -r 'Bun.serve' src` → no hits; src/server/index.ts imports fastify
- meta-check: tsconfig include covers src/**, so a deliberate type error in src FAILS `bun run check`
- an `@shared/*` import resolves under BOTH check and build; vite proxies /api → :8787
- pure scaffold: no behavior tests required (stated)
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator, 2026-07-02): `bun run check`→exit 0; `bun run build`→dist/index.html + assets, exit 0; `bun run test`→'No test files found, exiting with code 0' (passWithNoTests, no dummy test); server boot `curl localhost:8787/api/health`→{"ok":true}. Scope: 14 touched paths all ⊆ declared ∪ {package.json,bun.lock}; no drift. Gaming read: test script = 'vitest run --passWithNoTests' (real green-on-empty), tsconfig include=['src/**/*'] (meta-check holds), src/server/index.ts imports fastify, no Bun.serve. Needed one re-dispatch to make the test runner green-on-empty.

tickets.title: System prompt + renderLibrary (the product IP)
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/prompt.ts — THE product IP (§6.2). SYSTEM_PROMPT (frozen; never interpolate JD/timestamp) encodes: judge from FACTS not tags; the fact-lock (never invent, never strengthen — 'contributed to' ≠ 'led'); the rephrase policy full/light/none GENERATED from SECTIONS (so prompt/code never drift); rank-within-group = the lede; a leadRationale on each group's leading item tied to a NAMED JD signal; return ONLY the flat TailorDecision with every number grounded in facts. renderLibrary(entries) serializes deterministically (by section, then sortKey desc, then id; stable key order) and FOREGROUNDS facts (tags not presented as a scorable field). This prompt is what makes the §22 lede flip — if the eval (T014) doesn't flip sharply, THIS file is the bug, not the code.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Derivation not literal: the policy block is produced by a pure fn(SECTIONS); a test passing a SECTIONS copy with one section's rephrase FLIPPED asserts the rendered block reflects the flipped value (a hardcoded string fails this)
- Order-independence: SCRAMBLED input entries → output equals the canonical section→sortKey-desc→id ordering, and two different input orderings yield BYTE-IDENTICAL output
- Facts foregrounded: renderLibrary(e) output contains every string in e.facts; tags are not framed as 'match these'
- SYSTEM_PROMPT contains no JD/timestamp interpolation
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/prompt.test.ts 9/9. SYSTEM_PROMPT frozen (no JD/timestamp interpolation), encodes facts-not-tags + fact-lock ('contributed to'≠'led') + rank=lede + leadRationale-names-signal. Rephrase policy DERIVED from SECTIONS via pure fn (flip test: changing a section's rephrase in a SECTIONS copy changes the rendered block — not a hardcoded literal). renderLibrary deterministic (section→sortKey desc→id, byte-identical across input orderings), facts foregrounded, tags labeled 'never a relevance signal'. NOTE: real proof of the flip is the live eval T014.

tickets.title: assemble() — deterministic structure from the flat decision
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/assemble.ts: assemble(decision, entries, layout, sections): TailoredResume. Server owns ALL structure (§5): group by registry groupBy; group/flat order by registry `order` (recency = MAX member sortKey desc, server-computed; relevance = ascending min member rank; manual = sortKey); items-WITHIN-group ALWAYS by rank (lowest = lede); coerce text for rephrase:'none' to facts verbatim (empty-facts → text:''); filter to sections enabled in layout. The model NEVER controls structure. Keyless-testable — the regression net's core.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Max-member contrast: group A sortKeys [202101,202503] supplied in that order, group B [202501] → A ordered before B (A's key = max = 202503, distinguishing max from min/first)
- Coercion is an OVERRIDE not pass-through: a rephrase:'none' entry fed decision text:"FABRICATED" → output text is the verbatim fact, not "FABRICATED"; empty-facts entry → text:""
- Section order follows LAYOUT: layout orders B before A while entries inserted A before B → output sections [B,A]; an enabled:false section present in entries is absent
- items within a group order by rank (lowest first); flipping rank flips the leading item
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/assemble.test.ts 7/7. Server owns structure: recency groups by MAX member sortKey desc (contrast test distinguishes max from min/first); rephrase:'none' text COERCED to facts verbatim (decision text 'FABRICATED' overridden; empty-facts→''); section order follows LAYOUT (permuted) + disabled sections dropped; items within group by rank (flip test). Deterministic core of the keyless regression net.

tickets.title: validateNoFabrication + extractNumbers/hasNumberToken
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/validate.ts per §6.3: validateNoFabrication(resume, entries, baseSummary?) throws FabricationError on (a) an item entryId not a real entry, (b) a number in item.text not present in THAT item's OWN entry facts (per-entry scoped, not pooled), (c) a summary number not traceable to a kept entry's facts OR baseSummary. extractNumbers/hasNumberToken compare WHOLE number tokens INCLUDING attached %/k/x/units (so 30k≠30, 50%≠50), separators normalized (1,000==1000), NOT substrings ('1' ≠ inside '2021'). Mechanical only (§23) — NO LLM-checks-LLM pass, ever.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Token semantics (one assert per pair): text "30k" PASSES only if facts contain "30k", THROWS if facts contain only "30"; "50%" does NOT match a fact of "50"; "1,000" matches a fact of "1000"
- Per-entry scoping: an item whose number is present in a DIFFERENT entry's facts but absent from its OWN entry THROWS
- Detection completeness: a fake number injected into a DEEP location (summary, or a buried non-leading item in a late group) still THROWS and names the invented token (proves all items scanned)
- clean output PASSES; unknown entryId THROWS; summary number grounded in baseSummary PASSES
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/validate.test.ts 11/11. Whole-token semantics ('30k'≠'30','50%'≠'50','1,000'=='1000','1' not in '2021'); per-entry scoping enforced; deep-buried fake number throws naming the token; unknown entryId throws; baseSummary-grounded summary passes. Mechanical only, no LLM call.

tickets.title: Shared eval-core: hash(jd,entries), flip-predicate, canonical JDs, tag-shuffle
tickets.origin: red-team Finding A (fixture provenance) + Finding B (tag-scoring); spec §22
tickets.context: SINGLE SOURCE that closes the two structural holes. Author src/server/tailor/evalcore.ts exporting: (1) hashKey(jd, entries) — the ONE fixture key used by BOTH FixtureEngine (T010) and record-fixtures (T013), so a fixture can't be keyed one way and looked up another; (2) CONTRAST_JDS — the 3 §22 JDs, authored so NONE contains any entry id or a fact substring ≥ ~12 chars from its target (no keyword leakage), each mapped to its expected target (platform-sdk/rules-engine/frontend-rewrite); (3) flipPredicate(resume: TailoredResume, targetId) → { leads, rationaleNamesSignal } — target is its group's UNIQUE lowest-rank leading item AND its group.leadRationale contains a token from resume.signals; used identically by eval, record-fixtures, and the keyless suite; (4) tagShuffle(entries) — returns entries with tags permuted/stripped across entries (for the invariance control). This module is imported by T010, T013, T014, T015 — the provenance chain's spine.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- hashKey is deterministic and order-insensitive over entries; hashKey(CONTRAST_JDS[i], SEED_ENTRIES) is stable
- unit test: none of CONTRAST_JDS contains an entry id or a ≥12-char fact substring from its target (guards keyword leakage)
- flipPredicate returns leads=true only when target is the UNIQUE minimum-rank leading item of its group; false on a rank tie or when target is in cut
- tagShuffle preserves entry ids/facts/sortKeys and only permutes tags
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator): check/build green; test/evalcore.test.ts adds 14 (68 total). hashKey deterministic + order-insensitive (sha256 over jd+entries-sorted-by-id); ANTI-LEAKAGE verified — each CONTRAST_JDS jd contains neither its target id nor any ≥12-char substring of that target's facts (builder's drafts leaked 'integration path'/'the codebase'/'consistent patterns' and were reworded until clean). JDs read as realistic employer descriptions of the NEED, not keyword lists. flipPredicate: leads = target is its group's items[0] (leading) & not in cut; rationaleNamesSignal = leadRationale references a resume.signals token (true/false/filler/empty cases tested). tagShuffle permutes tags across entries, preserves id/facts/sortKey, no mutation. No new deps. Scope: only evalcore.ts + test.

tickets.title: TailorEngine (Fixture + Provider) + tailor() orchestration
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/engine.ts per §6.1: interface TailorEngine { decide(jd, entries): Promise<TailorDecision> }. ProviderEngine (real): resolveModel(cfg) then generateObject({ model, schema: TailorDecisionZ, system: SYSTEM_PROMPT + renderLibrary(entries), prompt: 'Tailor for this job description:\n\n'+jd, providerOptions: providerOptionsFor(provider) }); retry EXACTLY once on failure then throw. FixtureEngine (keyless): loadFixtureDecision by hashKey(jd, entries) FROM T016 (shared) out of test/fixtures/decisions; a MISS throws a no_fixture-tagged error (→422), NEVER a silent fallback / first-file. tailor(engine, jd, entries, layout, baseSummary?): decision = engine.decide → assemble → validateNoFabrication → return (engine-agnostic). Engine factory by env LEDE_TAILOR_ENGINE (live|fixture; fixture default under NODE_ENV=test). The engine split is what makes everything except the live call keyless.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Validate-wired contrast: tailor() with a stub engine returning a decision containing a FABRICATED number THROWS FabricationError; same stub with a clean decision returns a resume (proves validate runs, not just assemble)
- Hash keying with ≥2 fixtures: decide(jdA)→fixtureA, decide(jdB)→fixtureB (distinct); decide(jdC unrecorded) THROWS no_fixture WHILE fixtures exist (closes first-file/silent-fallback)
- Retry count: a mock model throwing once then succeeding → decide resolves after EXACTLY 2 calls; throwing twice → rejects after 2
- Engine factory returns FixtureEngine under NODE_ENV=test and ProviderEngine under live (assert by type); ProviderEngine calls generateObject with schema===TailorDecisionZ and a system string containing renderLibrary(entries) output
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator): check/build green; test/engine.test.ts 10 (78 total). tailor() = decide→assemble→validateNoFabrication (validate WIRED, contrast test: fabricated '9999%' throws FabricationError, clean returns resume). ProviderEngine retry EXACTLY once (try/catch/retry, generateObject ≤2 calls, asserted). FixtureEngine keys by evalcore.hashKey; MISS throws NoFixtureError code 'no_fixture' listing scenarios (no silent fallback / first-file). makeEngine: fixture under NODE_ENV=test/LEDE_TAILOR_ENGINE=fixture, ProviderEngine (google/gemini-2.5-flash) under live. Wiring test asserts generateObject called with schema===TailorDecisionZ + system containing renderLibrary output. Scope: only engine.ts+test.

tickets.title: record-fixtures script + recorded decision fixtures (KEY-GATED)

BEFORE writing each fixture it runs the decision through tailor()→assemble→validateNoFabrication AND T016 flipPredicate — a non-flipping or fabricating decision CANNOT be written. tickets.title: Phase 0 acceptance oracle — scripts/eval.ts (KEY-GATED, THE gate)
tickets.context: Author scripts/eval.ts — THE Phase 0 behavioral oracle (§22, §25). Constructs ProviderEngine EXPLICITLY (Google bootstrap key, model gemini-2.5-flash per ledger [v2-005] — pro is quota-blocked) and EXITS NON-ZERO if no key (never skip-as-pass, never fall back to FixtureEngine). Runs the live model over T016 CONTRAST_JDS against EXACTLY SEED_ENTRIES via tailor(), using T016 flipPredicate. Exit 0 iff all 3 flip correctly, non-zero (naming the miss) otherwise. Locked: if it doesn't flip sharply, the PROMPT (§6.2, T007) is wrong — not the code.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run scripts/eval.ts` (with GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY) exits 0; asserts engine instanceof ProviderEngine and ≥1 live response with token usage
- over exactly SEED_ENTRIES (length===3, ids match): for each JD the expected target is present in items (NOT cut) and is the STRICT UNIQUE minimum rank
- the three leading entryIds are MUTUALLY DISTINCT and equal the SET {cloudcase-platform-sdk, cloudcase-rules-engine, cloudcase-frontend-rewrite} (set assertion, not 3 isolated checks)
- each target's leadRationale contains a token from that JD's decision.signals; the three rationales are pairwise different
- TAG-SHUFFLE CONTROL (Finding B): re-run over tagShuffle(SEED_ENTRIES); the flip is UNCHANGED (if shuffling tags changes the lede → tag-scoring → FAIL)
- exits non-zero naming the failing JD when a lede does not flip
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator RAN THE GATE LIVE): `bun run scripts/eval.ts` → exit 0. BASE: all 3 JDs flip to correct target (platform-sdk/rules-engine/frontend-rewrite), leads+rationaleNamesSignal, mutually-distinct set. TAG-SHUFFLE: same 3 flips hold + same target per JD (fact-driven, not tag-driven). No-key run → exit 1 (refuses, no FixtureEngine fallback). Gaming read: uses ProviderEngine explicitly, EXPECTED_TARGETS as a Set, no fallback. THIS IS THE PHASE-0 §22/§25-Tier0 ORACLE PASSING. Scope: only scripts/eval.ts.

tickets.title: Server runs under Node (tsx), not Bun — better-sqlite3 native ABI; add real-boot smoke gate
tickets.origin: repair: bun runtime cannot dlopen better-sqlite3 native addon (escaped E1-B); ledger [v2-017]
tickets.context:
Baseline (oracle.md): bun run check + bun run build + bun run test pass, keyless. Touch ONLY declared files (+ package.json manifest). Add tests for new behavior.

ESCAPED ISSUE (verified by coordinator 2026-07-02): running `bun src/server/index.ts` dies with ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED — better-sqlite3 ships a Node-ABI prebuilt (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 127) that Bun 1.3.14's embedded V8 (137) cannot load; better-sqlite3 uses direct V8/NAN bindings, not N-API, so this is NOT rebuild-away (oven-sh/bun#4290). Under Node v22 via tsx the server boots, runs migrations, and GET /api/health returns {ok:true} (coordinator confirmed).
RESOLUTION (mechanical, ledger [v2-017]): the server PROCESS runs under Node — reverting the runner to spec §3.1's own 'Node >= 20'. Bun stays the package-manager + script runner (bun install / bun run check|build|test unchanged; they already use vitest/node).
CHANGES: in package.json scripts, change `start` from `bun src/server/index.ts` to `tsx src/server/index.ts`; change `dev:api` from `bun --watch src/server/index.ts` to `tsx watch src/server/index.ts`. Add `tsx` to devDependencies. Do NOT touch check/build/test scripts. Do NOT introduce bun:sqlite (it breaks vitest).

STRENGTHENED CHECK (escaped-bug rule): add test/boot.smoke.test.ts that spawns the REAL entrypoint under the actual runner (node via tsx — e.g. child_process spawn of `tsx src/server/index.ts`, or `node --import tsx`) against a fresh tmp DATA_DIR on a free port; poll GET /api/health until {ok:true} (fail on ~15s timeout); assert the .sqlite file was created under DATA_DIR; tear the child down. This is the boot gate the baseline lacked — it catches a runner that can't load the native driver.

- package.json `start` and `dev:api` no longer invoke `bun` on src/server/index.ts (grep: no `bun` invoking src/server/index.ts in scripts); `tsx` present in devDependencies
- new test in test/boot.smoke.test.ts

FOUNDATION for Phase 2 (everything else depends on this).
(1) src/server/crypto.ts (node:crypto only): `encrypt(plaintext, masterKey)` -> {iv, tag, ciphertext} using AES-256-GCM with a FRESH random iv each call; `decrypt({iv,tag,ciphertext}, masterKey)` -> plaintext (throws on tampered tag/wrong key); `hashPassword(pw)` -> {hash, salt} using scrypt (random salt); `verifyPassword(pw, {hash,salt})` -> bool (timing-safe compare). masterKey is the decoded 32 bytes.
(2) src/server/config.ts: EXTEND loadConfig() to parse+VALIDATE `masterKey` (from LEDE_MASTER_KEY, base64 decoding to EXACTLY 32 bytes — else throw), `sessionSecret` (from LEDE_SESSION_SECRET, non-empty — @fastify/secure-session needs >=32 chars, validate), `authDisabled` (LEDE_AUTH_DISABLED === 'true', default false). loadConfig() FAIL-FAST throws a clear error if master key or session secret is missing/malformed. Do NOT auto-generate a master key. Keep existing port/tailorEngine/dataDir.
(3) test/setup.ts (NEW vitest global setup) + wire it in vite.config.ts `test.setupFiles`: set process.env.LEDE_MASTER_KEY (a valid 32-byte base64), LEDE_SESSION_SECRET (a >=32-char test value), and LEDE_AUTH_DISABLED='true' BEFORE tests run, so the whole existing keyless suite (buildApp etc.) still boots. (The auth guard is proven ENABLED separately in E2-B.)
(4) test/boot.smoke.test.ts (EXTEND, owned-contract now changes): the spawned real entrypoint must now receive LEDE_MASTER_KEY + LEDE_SESSION_SECRET in its env (add them to the existing happy-path spawn). ADD the process-level boot-refusal cases: spawn the real entrypoint with LEDE_MASTER_KEY UNSET, and separately with a MALFORMED master key (not 32 bytes) -> the process exits NON-ZERO, never starts listening, and does NOT write/auto-generate a master key anywhere.
RED-TEAM acceptance encodes: master-key load-bearing (distinct IVs; wrong-key decrypt throws) + process boot refusal.

RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) DOM-ABSENCE not display:none — feed a fixture with NON-EMPTY leadRationale + cut reasons, assert those exact strings are absent from the ACTUAL print-target subtree's textContent (a display:none-only impl FAILS; render the real print root, not ResumePage in isolation); (2) TIMEBOX ceiling — text callouts only, GREP-FORBID EventSource/SSE (non-MVP §15), animation libs (framer-motion), any new tailors/history table; cap reasoning-UI ticket count; (3) remaining sections — feed one entry of EACH new section and assert each renders with its registry label/order, and empty-facts entries (cert/reference) render from meta with text:''.
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 3 (merged-tree): ReasoningPanel renders signals + per-group leadRationale + cut; leadRationale/cut ABSENT from the printed DOM tree (not merely hidden); remaining sections render from data.

tickets.title: Phase 4 — render polish + Docker/compose/README (self-host)
tickets.context: COARSE EPIC — decompose after E3 into: (a) print.css polish to ATS-safe (§10: single-column, #000/#fff, no tables/images/multi-column/borders, system serif/sans NOT Plex); (b) §12 design finish — tokens.css, shadcn theme mapped to tokens, self-hosted IBM Plex via @fontsource, low radius, --accent primary; (c) Dockerfile multi-stage (build SPA+server → slim BUN runtime image; deviation from §19 node-slim per toolchain decision) + docker-compose.yml (one service, volume at DATA_DIR) + .env.example with the two required secrets; migrations on boot; (d) README self-host first-run. RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) DEEP boot check — after compose up, migrations ran (tables in the mounted volume) and GET / returns the built index.html (not a stub); (2) carry boot-refusal into the image — compose up with the two secrets ABSENT → container exits non-zero; NO real secret baked in image/compose (grep layers+compose; .env.example placeholders only); (3) VOLUME persistence — write data, compose down && up, data survives; (4) fonts self-hosted — grep build output for fonts.googleapis/CDN font URLs → ABSENT; @fontsource present; (5) print.css grep-checkable (no / in ResumePage, no multi-column in print target, #000/#fff) but TRUE ATS text-extraction/reading-order needs headless print — flag as only partially machine-checkable, requires manual/browser verification (state honestly).
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 4 (merged-tree): print.css ATS-safe (grep + honest manual caveat); §12 tokens+shadcn+self-hosted Plex; `docker compose up` deep-boots with the two secrets (migrations ran, real SPA served), refuses without them, volume persists; README first-run documented.
